experiment: Abhi vs the course — simulated $5,000 prop account

pending_orders.note: 4-week base 716.58–755.42 (5.4% range) near highs; entry on break of 755.42; fill requires volume ≥ 87,934,484 (1.5× avg) — a quiet break is not the setup.
4-week base 89.26–97.52 (9.3% range) near highs; entry on break of 97.52; fill requires volume ≥ 8,630,700 (1.5× avg) — a quiet break is not the setup.

sessions.regime: Risk-off tech day: SPY ~-0.7%, QQQ ~-2.3%, Nasdaq -1.4%, chips heavy (NVDA ~-1.9%). Profit-taking in tech on mixed economic news.
sessions.actions: No trades. Two reasons logged: (1) red impulse day is the wrong day to buy a pullback before any sign of a reclaim — setup requires the turn, not the fall; (2) exact daily levels could not be verified from free feeds today, and the charter says no numbers, no trade.
sessions.brutality_note: Mild for us (flat). Rough for anyone long chips on margin.
lessons: Sitting out IS a position — day 1 of most real prop evaluations is lost by forcing action onto a scoreboard.
Data quality gates trades: an entry without a verified level is a guess wearing a number.
A -2% QQQ day starts the pullback clock: if the dip lands on rising 20/50-day averages and reclaims, that's the first legitimate setup of the experiment.

sessions.actions: SPY base_breakout order placed: entry 755.49, stop 715.15, target 836.17, 1 shares; QQQ base_breakout order placed: entry 746.2, stop 699.51, target 839.58, 1 shares.
sessions.brutality_note: Tape moved 1.1% on average; we were flat. Sitting out is a position.
lessons: Orders placed, none filled yet — patience between signal and fill is part of the edge.
